risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: The project faces persistent substantial political and governance risks that constrain implementation progress. The document maintains a 'Substantial' rating for Political and Governance risk across all review periods, indicating ongoing fragility in the operating environment that affects project execution.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: Substantial; Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 07 - 30 Jan 2025: Substantial; Proposed Rating: Substantial

risk_title: Governance Fragility Impacting DisCo Institutional Capacity
risk_summary: Multiple governance and anti-corruption framework requirements remain incomplete and are conditional on PforR on-lending agreement execution. The delays in establishing governance mechanisms suggest underlying institutional fragility within the distribution companies and their oversight bodies.
evidence_quote: Ensure existing governance and anti-corruption framework incorporate the Program... DISCOs confirm to PMU that their fraud and corruption / complaints redress mechanism have incorporated the DISREP program within the specified time period... Under preparation and will be condition to sign PforR on-lending agreements with DisCos.

risk_title: Delays in Fiduciary and Governance Clearances Affecting Program Execution
risk_summary: Critical fiduciary systems and governance frameworks required prior to PforR on-lending remain under preparation. These delays indicate governance capacity constraints and potential tensions between the World Bank and implementing entities regarding institutional readiness.
evidence_quote: While initial delays were experienced due to prolonged consultations and approvals, significant progress has been made. The necessary legal clearances have been obtained, and the finalized agreement has been shared with the DisCos for endorsement.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints in Program Implementation Unit
risk_summary: The PIU has experienced staffing disruptions with withdrawal of a previously selected candidate, requiring reinitiation of recruitment. This reflects institutional fragility and capacity constraints that could affect program coordination and governance.
evidence_quote: The recruitment process for key positions within the Program Implementation Unit (PIU) is being reinitiated following the withdrawal of a previously selected candidate. The process is expected to resume in August 2025 to ensure the unit is adequately staffed to support implementation.
